survivors_flagged.one_line: SoftBank to acquire for $16.00 cash; shareholder vote passed Apr 23 2026, expected close 2H26 — trades ~$15.78 (~1.4% gross spread), clean dated merger-arb.
Viaskin Peanut toddler BLA submission targeted 2H 2026 under accelerated pathway — a dated regulatory milestone but a filing, not yet an asymmetric decision; one real catalyst.
DFH is live acquirer in a public, contested all-cash bid for Beazer ($25.75/sh, ~$704M, ~40% premium); structural M&A still unresolved — own the consolidation optionality.
DT120 Breakthrough-designated; Phase 3 GAD readouts (Voyage early-Q3, Panorama late-Q3 2026) still forward — dated binaries; Emerge MDD already positive (partially fired).
Dated $6B TAE Technologies fusion merger expected to close later 2026 (SEC/shareholder votes pending) plus mooted Truth Social spin-off; complicated, high deal-risk binary on a crypto-treasury shell.
ReMEDy2 Phase 2/3 stroke interim analysis on track for Q4 2026, but it only re-sizes the trial (sample-size pick) rather than delivering topline efficacy; modest asymmetry.
Positive RESTORE-FA Friedreich ataxia data (May-2026, FXN +65%); registrational-path update guided Q4 2026 — one real dated biotech catalyst.
Two FDA accelerated-approval submissions (DMD z-rostudirsen Q2-2026, DM1) + Q1-2027 DM1/DMD readouts; Breakthrough designations — real dated biotech binaries.
Signed $210/sh take-private by PIF/Silver Lake/Affinity ($55B LBO); shareholders approved Dec-2025, expected close Q1 FY2027 — a live, dated merger-arb (spread/close-risk asymmetry remains).

Live hostile target — GameStop/Ryan Cohen $125/sh bid (May-3, rejected May-12), holds 7.8%, taking it to shareholders; ongoing Form 425s into late-Jun 2026 — real mispriced contested-control situation.
Definitive Apollo take-private at $5.03 cash (signed May 2026), Onex 90%+ approved by written consent so no vote needed, $84M reciprocal break fee, 2H26 close - clean dated arb with small spread.
Enliven: ELVN-001 CML; FDA-aligned 80mg dose, pivotal ENABLE-2 Phase-3 starts H2 2026 + further Ph1 data — forward clinical catalysts, but no dated PDUFA and not a mispriced hard binary.

verified.catalyst: SoftBank to acquire DigitalBridge for $16.00/share cash ($4B EV); shareholder vote passed Apr 23 2026, now pending regulatory clearances and 85% client-revenue consent, expected close 2H26.
verified.dated_milestone: Regulatory approvals (CFIUS/FERC/FCC/MAS/UK FCA) + 85% client consent → expected close 2H26; outside date Mar 29 2027.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: SoftBank Group Corp.) + G2 (concrete commitment: signed merger agreement, $16.00 cash, vote passed) + G3 (specific figure: residual ~$0.225/sh = ~1.4% gross spread to deal price)
verified.edge: L — deal is already heavily re-rated; at $15.78 the ~1.4% gross spread over 6+ months annualizes to only mid-single-digit %, thin compensation for CFIUS/FCC foreign-investment tail risk on a Japanese buyer of US digital infrastructure.
verified.primary_source: DigitalBridge IR / Businesswire "Stockholders Approve Acquisition by SoftBank" (2026-04-23); FMP DBRG quote $15.775 (2026-06-29); MLex on CFIUS/FERC/FCC requirements.
verified.analysis: The merger is real, dated, and forward-looking: SoftBank's $16.00 cash bid for DBRG cleared the shareholder vote Apr 23 2026 (verified via DigitalBridge IR + Businesswire), and the live tape confirms a tight ~1.4% gross spread (FMP quote $15.775 on 2026-06-29). All three Bloom gates pass on substance, but the catalyst is largely priced in — the only remaining return is the narrow arb capture, which is low-edge given the multi-agency regulatory stack (CFIUS, FERC, FCC, MAS, UK FCA per MLex) and the inherent foreign-acquirer scrutiny risk. This is a clean but unexciting arb: WATCH rather than ACTIVE because the risk-adjusted spread is thin and the deal could push to the Mar 2027 outside date.

verified.catalyst: DBV Technologies targets submitting the Viaskin Peanut patch BLA for toddlers (1-3 yrs) under the FDA Accelerated Approval pathway in 2H 2026 (end of 2026), following its 4-7yr BLA filing due end-June 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: Toddler (1-3yr) BLA submission targeted 2H 2026 / end of 2026; nearer 4-7yr BLA targeted end of June 2026 (next date: ~Q4 2026 for toddler filing)
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete dated regulatory commitment — a BLA filing, not an FDA decision)
verified.edge: L — a BLA submission is a company-controlled administrative milestone, not the asymmetric FDA verdict; it is largely de-risked/expected after positive EPITOPE+VITESSE+COMFORT data, so low surprise. The true binary (approval vs CRL) lands ~6-10mo post-acceptance, into 2027, beyond this catalyst window.
verified.primary_source: DBV Technologies Q1 2026 Financial Results press release, April 30, 2026 (toddler BLA "second half of this year"); corroborated by The Stock Observer, May 22, 2026 (CEO Tassé: first BLA by end of June, toddler pathway end of 2026)
verified.analysis: Triage characterization is accurate: this is a real, dated, forward regulatory milestone but a filing event, not an asymmetric decision. There are actually two stacked catalysts — the 4-7yr BLA due end-June 2026 (imminent, possibly filing now) and the toddler 1-3yr Accelerated-Approval BLA targeted 2H 2026; both are submissions DBV controls, broadly anticipated after strong Phase 3 data (VITESSE 46.6% vs 14.8%; EPITOPE), with Breakthrough Therapy + intended Priority Review. Cash of $229M (3/31/26) funds operations into Q2 2027, covering both filings, so it is not a financing/macro bet — but the genuine asymmetric event (FDA approval/CRL) sits beyond the filing, making this WATCH-grade dated milestone rather than an ACTIVE binary.

verified.catalyst: DFH is the unsolicited bidder for Beazer at $25.75/sh cash (~$704M), a proposal Beazer's board has now rejected three times with no live forward milestone.
verified.dated_milestone: None outstanding — Beazer's annual meeting already passed (Feb 2026, poison-pill/rights agreement renewed); no proxy contest, consent solicitation, tender offer, or deadline filed as of 2026-06-29. Next BZH FQ3 earnings (~late Jul/Aug 2026) is the only scheduled date, and is not a deal trigger.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 met (named counterparty Beazer) and a specific figure exists ($25.75 vs ~$41.83 book), but G2 fails — proposal is explicitly non-binding with no signed agreement/committed financing; no DATED FORWARD milestone exists.
verified.edge: L — fully public and already re-rated; bid news fired May 5-20 2026 (~6 wks stale), three rejections + entrenched poison pill, declining price ($28.50→$29.00→$25.75) signals a stalling, not progressing, situation. Merger-arb optionality lives in the TARGET (BZH), not in DFH the acquirer; owning DFH for "consolidation optionality" is a deploy-$704M-cash bet whose accretion is speculative and actively blocked.
verified.primary_source: Beazer Homes 8-K / press release rejecting DFH proposals, businesswire 2026-05-11; DFH reaffirmation investor presentation 2026-05-20; National Mortgage News 2026-05-11/13
verified.analysis: DFH is the hostile acquirer, not the target, so the merger-arb spread sits in BZH; for DFH the "catalyst" is the speculative chance it wins a deal Beazer's board has unanimously rejected three times and structurally defended via a renewed NOL rights agreement (poison pill) approved at the Feb-2026 annual meeting. There is no dated forward milestone — no proxy contest, consent solicitation, tender offer, or deadline has been launched, so the situation is open-ended and the bid itself is already public and digested (fired May 5-20, 2026). Fails the strict gate on DATED + FORWARD and is arguably on the wrong ticker for the stated thesis; this is consolidation optionality with no clock, not a live un-fired catalyst.

verified.catalyst: Two forward Phase 3 GAD topline readouts for DT-120 ODT (lysergide) — Voyage and Panorama — are due in Q3 2026, with FDA Breakthrough Therapy designation already in hand for GAD.
verified.dated_milestone: Voyage GAD topline early Q3 2026 (next ~July/Aug); Panorama GAD topline late Q3 2026 (~Sept 2026)
verified.bloom_gates: G3 — two specific dated Phase 3 GAD topline readouts (Voyage fully enrolled n=214, HAM-A Wk12 primary; Panorama n=200), each a discrete unpriced binary on an FDA Breakthrough-designated asset
verified.edge: M — material dated binaries on a $4.9B clinical name, but the stock already ~3.5x'd ($12.88 low to ~$45.3, 50/200-day avg ~$14) on the correlated June 22 positive Emerge MDD readout, so the same-molecule GAD reads are partly de-risked/priced; readout asymmetry (failure downside) remains the live edge
verified.primary_source: Definium/MindMed IR press release "Positive Topline Results from Phase 3 Emerge Study" (2026-06-22) + StockTitan DT-120 readout map (2026-04-22); FMP quote 2026-06-29
verified.kill_fact: Primary sources confirm all load-bearing facts. Definium (fmrly MindMed, MNMD->DFTX) IR/BusinessWire: Voyage GAD n=214 enrollment complete, topline early 3Q2026, HAM-A Wk12 primary (Ph3 mirrors Ph2b 12-wk design); Panorama GAD late 3Q2026, enrollment complete (target n=200); FDA Breakthrough Therapy designation for GAD confirmed. Emerge MDD fired POSITIVE 2026-06-22 (8.1-pt placebo-adj MADRS Wk6, p<0.0001), correctly treated as partially-fired. Re-rate verified: 50d avg $14.26 -> ~$45.29 (~3.2x), yearHigh $47.01 (within ~4% of 52wk high), yearLow $12.88. Both GAD reads are genuine forward unfired binaries; post-MDD run-up caps unpriced edge. Score 6/ACTIVE holds.
verified.analysis: DT-120 ODT (lysergide) has two forward, dated Phase 3 GAD topline readouts — Voyage early-Q3 2026 (fully enrolled, n=214, HAM-A Wk12 primary) and Panorama late-Q3 2026 — both unfired binaries on an FDA Breakthrough-designated indication. The Emerge MDD leg already fired positive on 2026-06-22 (8.1-pt placebo-adjusted MADRS at Wk6, p<0.0001), driving a ~3.5x re-rate to near a 52-week high, so it is correctly treated as partially fired and the GAD upside is partly priced. Net: genuine near-term dated clinical catalysts justify ACTIVE, but the post-MDD run-up caps the unpriced edge to Medium with meaningful downside if either GAD read disappoints.

verified.catalyst: DJT's $6B all-stock merger with fusion developer TAE Technologies, targeting a Q4 2026 close pending an S-4 registration and TMTG shareholder / TAE consent votes.
verified.dated_milestone: S-4 filing then effectiveness (announced, not yet filed as of June 2026) -> shareholder/consent vote -> deal close targeted Q4 2026 or sooner
verified.instrument: equity (DJT common); merger consideration is all-stock
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: TAE Technologies) + G2 (concrete commitment: definitive all-stock merger, up to $300M cash funding to TAE, ~50/50 ownership split, Q4 2026 target close)
verified.edge: L — deal is heavily covered and already re-rated on the Dec 18 2025 +33% announcement; close is distant (Q4 2026) with the S-4 not even filed yet, the Truth Social spin-off optionality was abandoned June 10 2026, and fusion commercialization (first electricity ~2031) makes the post-deal valuation a speculative crypto-treasury-plus-fusion-shell story rather than a tight, near-dated unpriced spread.
verified.primary_source: Trump Media & TAE Technologies merger update / Form 425, June 10 2026 (stocktitan/Yahoo Finance); original deal CNBC Dec 18 2025
verified.analysis: DJT signed a definitive ~$6B all-stock merger with fusion company TAE Technologies (announced Dec 18 2025), reaffirmed June 10 2026 with a Q4 2026 close target, an S-4/proxy still pending, and the previously mooted Truth Social spin-off now dropped. The catalyst is real, dated, forward and unfired (a binary on votes/regulatory approval), passing Bloom G1 (TAE counterparty) and G2 (definitive agreement + up to $300M funding commitment). But it scores low: the headline already re-rated the stock, the close is far off with the registration statement not yet filed, the spin-off optionality is gone, and the underlying asset is a pre-revenue fusion developer (electricity ~2031) bolted onto a crypto-treasury shell trading near 52-week lows — high deal/dilution risk with no tight unpriced spread to harvest. WATCH, not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: ReMEDy2 Phase 2/3 acute ischemic stroke DSMB interim analysis (sample-size re-estimation 300-728 pts + futility stop), anticipated before year-end 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: Interim analysis anticipated Q4 2026 / before year-end 2026 (next date: Q4 2026); triggered at 200-patient enrollment, was 75% there as of May 20, 2026.
verified.bloom_gates: None cleanly met. Milestone is DATED + FORWARD but fails G1 (no named counterparty), G2 (no concrete commitment), G3 (no specific currently-unpriced figure — interim yields only a sample-size pick or a futility stop, not an efficacy/value number).
verified.edge: L — the interim re-sizes the trial rather than reading out efficacy; only binary outcomes are continue-with-sample-size (no upside signal) or stop-for-futility (downside-only), so asymmetry is negatively skewed, not unpriced upside. Topline efficacy is a 2027+ event.
verified.primary_source: DiaMedica press release / StockTitan + BioSpace, "75% Enrollment Milestone in ReMEDy2," dated 2026-05-20; FMP quote 2026-06-29.
verified.analysis: DMAC's only near-dated event is the Q4 2026 ReMEDy2 DSMB interim, which per the May 20, 2026 release evaluates sample-size re-estimation (300-728 patients) and a futility-stop rule — it explicitly does NOT deliver topline efficacy. A "continue" decision merely confirms the trial proceeds (no value-revealing figure), while the only sharp move risk is a futility stop, making this a negatively-skewed clinical-process milestone rather than an unpriced upside binary. Real and dated, but it fails the forward-asymmetry test: WATCH, not ACTIVE, with efficacy readout pushed to 2027+.

verified.catalyst: Positive RESTORE-FA Phase 1/2 data (FXN mRNA +65%, mFARS +6.4 pts) already released May 18 2026; only remaining forward item is a vague "registrational-path strategy update" guided for Q4 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: Q4 2026 registrational-path/strategy update (no specific date, no FDA meeting date disclosed)
verified.bloom_gates: none — fails G1 (no named counterparty/FDA meeting date), G2 (no concrete dated commitment, just "plan to update strategy"), G3 (no specific currently-unpriced figure)
verified.edge: L — the binary data event already fired and re-rated the stock (+27% on the print); price $13.96 sits ~4x off the $3.33 yr-low, above 50d ($12.45) and 200d ($9.78), and Piper Sandler already lifted PT to $20 post-data. The data is priced; cash into 2029 removes a financing catalyst. No remaining unpriced dated edge.
verified.primary_source: Design Therapeutics PR / GlobeNewswire & curefa.org: "Four-Week IV Data from RESTORE-FA Trial of DT-216P2," May 18, 2026; FMP quote DSGN $13.96 (2026-06-29)
verified.analysis: The triage catalyst conflates a FIRED event with a forward one: the RESTORE-FA readout (FXN +65%, mFARS +6.4 at 1 mpk) was announced May 18, 2026 and drove a +27% pop, with Piper Sandler then raising its PT to $20 — so it is already re-rated, not forward. The sole remaining future item is a soft "we intend to pursue a registrational path and will update strategy in Q4 2026" guidance, which has no named counterparty, no fixed date, and no concrete unpriced figure, so it fails a strict Bloom forward-catalyst gate. With shares near the post-data level (~$14 vs $3.33 yr-low) and cash funded into 2029, there is no dated, specific, currently-unpriced forward catalyst — tier NONE.

verified.catalyst: Dyne Therapeutics has a stacked neuromuscular regulatory binary: DMD z-rostudirsen BLA under FDA review (Priority Review requested) plus DM1 z-basivarsen ACHIEVE registrational topline due Q1-2027.
verified.dated_milestone: DM1 ACHIEVE registrational-expansion-cohort topline data: Q1-2027 (next hard date). Nearer: FDA filing/Priority-Review decision on the DMD BLA ~Q3-2026 (~60-day filing review from May 26, 2026 submission); DMD PDUFA action date not yet set pending acceptance; targeted DMD launch Q1-2027.
verified.bloom_gates: G3 (specific dated regulatory binaries: FDA filing/Priority-Review decision, eventual PDUFA approval/CRL, and DM1 Q1-2027 registrational readout — each an unpriced binary outcome); partial G2 (concrete committed BLA filing + $400M non-dilutive debt facility funding launches)
verified.edge: M — the flagship DMD submission catalyst flagged in triage already fired (BLA filed 2026-05-26, partial re-rate); the live forward edge is the FDA filing/Priority-Review decision (~Q3-2026) and eventual PDUFA approval (date not yet pinned), plus the DM1 ACHIEVE Q1-2027 readout. Accelerated-approval-on-dystrophin-surrogate carries genuine ODAC/CRL risk and the PDUFA date is unquantified, so not ACTIVE.
verified.primary_source: Dyne Therapeutics press release "Submission of BLA to U.S. FDA for Z-Rostudirsen in Exon 51 DMD" (2026-05-26, globenewswire/investors.dyne-tx.com) + Q1-2026 8-K business update (DM1 ACHIEVE REC topline Q1-2027, DMD launch Q1-2027)
verified.analysis: DYN is Dyne Therapeutics; the triage-flagged "Q2-2026 DMD submission" has now FIRED — z-rostudirsen (DYNE-251) BLA was submitted to FDA on May 26, 2026 with Priority Review and Accelerated Approval requested, so that specific catalyst is no longer forward. Genuine forward dated binaries remain: an FDA filing-acceptance/Priority-Review decision expected ~Q3-2026, the eventual PDUFA approval/CRL (date not yet public, targeting Q1-2027 launch), and the DM1 z-basivarsen ACHIEVE registrational-expansion-cohort topline planned for Q1-2027 to support a Q3-2027 BLA. These are real, specific, currently-unpriced regulatory binaries (Bloom G3), not macro — WATCH-grade, but edge is tempered because the headline submission already de-risked/re-rated and no hard PDUFA date is yet fixed.
